articles.summary: Creative-by-committee is a recipe for blandness - and who's got time to create anything when your calendar is back-to-back, anyway?

articles.title: Fewer bottlenecks. Faster launches. Start Looming
GLOBAL GROWTH REMAINS SLUGGISH AS TARIFF THREATS LOOM
articles.summary: Global economic growth is expected to slow as trade tensions and protectionist policies weigh on output. The International Monetary Fund (IMF) projects global growth will fall to 3.2% in 2025 and 3.1% in 2026, down from 3.3% last year. The US is forecast to slow to 2% growth this year from 2.8% in 2024, while China's growth is expected to drop to 4.8% and continue falling to 4.2% in 2026. The IMF warned that rising tariffs could fuel inflation in the US as higher import costs begin to reach consumers.

articles.title: RAGEBAIT IS EVERYWHERE. SHOULD MARKETERS LEAN IN?
THE OPTIMAL EMAIL MARKETING FREQUENCY
articles.summary: Sending 7 emails monthly is the optimal frequency for maximizing customer lifetime value. Reducing this to 4 emails cut lifetime profit by 32%, while increasing it to 10 emails reduced profit by 16%. The best results come from tailoring email frequency by purchase behavior, sending 5-7 emails to frequent buyers, 6-10 to medium buyers, and 12-14 to infrequent buyers. Frequent buyers tend to ignore more emails, while occasional buyers engage more when they receive additional messages.

articles.title: AI IS NOW LOOKING FOR THIS
articles.summary: AI citations are now on average 393 days newer than Google search results. Content with a recent date in the title or offer name is 50% more likely to appear in AI results. Update your reports, webinars, podcasts, guides, and blog titles. Instead of “The State of HR Laws”, use “The State of HR Laws – Q4 2025”. Add timestamps in your copy and refresh old content with current context.

articles.title: DOES TRENDJACKING WORK?
articles.summary: Trendjacking can deliver short-term brand relevance when it reflects the values and behaviors of your audience. Before joining a trend, ask whether it fits your brand's industry and whether your audience will care. Arby's capitalized on the viral sea shanty moment on TikTok to promote its fish sandwich, combining cultural relevance with humor. Strategic use of trend hashtags helped extend its reach.

articles.title: HOW ATLASSIAN USED LOOM TO REDUCE A CAMPAIGN LAUNCH FROM 90 TO 39 DAYS
articles.summary: When Loom's marketing team needed to launch a brand campaign fast, they ate their own dog food. Learn how they used Loom [10] for async video briefs, stakeholder reviews, and feedback loops instead of waiting for calendars to sync - and what normally took a full quarter

articles.title: got done in 39 days. Read how they did it
WALMART IS INCORPORATING CHATGPT INTO ONLINE SHOPPING. HERE'S HOW.
articles.summary: Walmart is partnering with OpenAI to integrate ChatGPT into its online shopping platform, allowing customers to search for products, receive recommendations, and complete purchases through AI-powered chat. The collaboration will also use OpenAI's Instant Checkout to let shoppers buy items directly within the chatbot. Walmart said the system will become more proactive as it learns customer preferences, aiming to make shopping faster and more personalized. A Coresight Research survey found 64% of consumers already use, plan to use, or want to use generative AI for shopping, and 29% are more likely to shop on sites with an AI chatbot.

articles.title: 2025 INSTAGRAM CONTENT PLAYBOOK
articles.summary: An analysis of 700M IG posts from January to June 2025 showed that posting frequency is the strongest predictor of account growth across all follower tiers. Reels drive reach for smaller accounts under 50K followers, while carousels outperform Reels in engagement for larger accounts, with the gap widening for accounts over 500K followers. Optimal posting is 8 PM on Wednesday and Friday. Trial Reels allow safe testing, scheduling maintains consistency, and influencer collaborations generate three times more engagement than brand posts.

articles.title: HOW TO KNOW IF YOUR GEO IS WORKING
articles.summary: Generative Engine Optimization rewards brands that are easy for AI tools to recommend in commercial moments. Track four specific outcomes to understand if your GEO strategy is working: share of search reflects how well your brand stands out, buyer-intent traffic shows whether that visibility leads to action, prompt visibility across AI tools signals credibility and brand strength, and conversational queries measure how effectively content drives qualified traffic.

articles.title: CORPORATE PRESSURE KILLED LULULEMON'S CREATIVE SOUL
articles.summary: Lululemon's founder, Chip Wilson, used a full-page Wall Street Journal ad to call out the company's current marketing strategy. In his critique, Wilson blamed the board for dismantling the company's creative DNA and diluting what made the brand distinctive. He cited a loss of product focus, brand differentiation, entrepreneurial drive, and creative leadership. When growth outpaces brand identity, companies lose pricing power and relevance. Wilson's call to reverse what he calls “GAP-ivization” is a reminder to protect what made a brand valuable in the first place.

articles.title: INSIDE ADVERTISING'S MOST GRUELING NEW GENRE: ‘YOU HAVE TO HAVE ZERO SOCIAL ANXIETY'
articles.summary: 203 Media is building a profitable marketing niche with unscripted street interview ads, offering brands a human antidote to AI-generated content. Founder Josh Suggs leads a team that captures raw reactions to consumer products in public spaces, then packages them into native-style ad creatives for platforms like TikTok and Instagram. The model works best for challenger brands looking to build trust fast. The format is high-effort but high-impact, requiring skilled interviewers with relentless hustle and zero social anxiety.

articles.title: STRAVA'S IPO PLANS REFLECT DIRECTION OF SOCIAL MOMENTUM
articles.summary: Strava's sustained user growth signals the rise of social media centered on real-world connection rather than content.
